grounding_source_rows: taxonomy Premises/Products/Aviation/Maritime descriptions; paper injury_location mode
grounding_note: Injury intakes name the harm but not the legal mechanism: dangerous property condition (Premises), defective product (Products), common-carrier context (Aviation/Maritime), or plain negligence (PI). The mechanism fact — what actually failed — is knowable by the user and selects the label.

scenarios.opening_query: I was seriously burned at a restaurant and I'm still doing wound care two months later.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Premises Liability || General Litigation > Products Liability
scenarios.hidden_fact: The tabletop butane burner exploded — same model recalled last year for valve failures; the restaurant kept using them.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Their tabletop butane burner exploded — a model recalled last year for valve failures, and they kept using them.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a defective product caused the burn
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: A server spilled a flaming dessert on me after slipping on a floor they'd been warned about all night.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: A server slipped on a floor they'd been warned about all night and spilled a flaming dessert on me.

scenarios.opening_query: My mother was hurt on her dream cruise and the company is hiding behind fine print.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || General Litigation > Maritime
scenarios.hidden_fact: It happened at sea two days out — the ticket's maritime forum and one-year limitation clauses are exactly why we need a maritime lawyer fast.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It happened at sea, two days out — the ticket's maritime forum and one-year deadline clauses are the problem.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the injury occurred at sea under maritime law
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It happened in the cruise line's shore terminal before boarding — an unmarked wet ramp on land.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was in the shore terminal before boarding — an unmarked wet ramp on land.
scenarios.vetting_note: At-sea vs on-land selects maritime's special deadlines.

scenarios.opening_query: I was injured on a small charter flight and the operator won't even return our calls.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || General Litigation > Aviation
scenarios.hidden_fact: The injury came from a hard emergency landing after engine trouble — NTSB is investigating the aircraft and operator, which makes it an aviation case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was a hard emergency landing after engine trouble — the NTSB is investigating, so it's an aviation case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an aviation accident and investigation are involved
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The flight itself was fine — their ground crew dropped the airstair while I was on it at the hangar.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The flight was fine — their ground crew dropped the airstair with me on it.
scenarios.vetting_note: In-flight accident vs ground negligence at the same operator.

scenarios.opening_query: My son's little league coach let the kids practice through a lightning warning and my son is lucky to be alive.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the city recreation league on a city field with a city-employee coordinator — the tort-claim notice window is already running.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the city rec league — city field, city coordinator — and the tort-claim notice window is running.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the league is municipally operated
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a private club league with volunteer parent coaches and its own insurance.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a private club league — volunteer coaches, their own insurance.
scenarios.vetting_note: Operator identity again in a youth-sports frame.

scenarios.opening_query: A ladder failed under my husband while he was cleaning our gutters and he broke both wrists.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || General Litigation > Products Liability
scenarios.hidden_fact: The brand-new ladder's rivets sheared at half its rated load — we kept it, and a testing lab confirmed the manufacturing defect.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The rivets sheared at half the rated load on a brand-new ladder — a lab confirmed the manufacturing defect, and we kept it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a product defect is documented
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The ladder was fine — our neighbor's dog charged him mid-climb and knocked it over; it's about the owner's control of the dog.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The ladder was fine — the neighbor's dog charged and knocked it over.

scenarios.opening_query: I collapsed at the gym from something they should have prevented and woke up in the ER.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Premises Liability || General Litigation > General Torts/Privacy || General Litigation > Personal Injury
scenarios.hidden_fact: What they 'should have prevented' is the trainer secretly filming in the sauna — I collapsed from heat while he blocked the door making a video; the filming and confinement are the real claims.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The trainer was secretly filming in the sauna and blocked the door while I overheated — the filming and confinement are the real claims.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether intentional privacy/confinement torts underlie the injury
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The sauna thermostat was broken and locked at maximum — a maintenance failure they'd logged twice that week.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The sauna thermostat was broken at maximum — logged twice that same week.

scenarios.opening_query: The playground equipment at our apartment complex collapsed with my daughter on it.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Premises Liability || Real Property > Tenant (Residential)
scenarios.hidden_fact: She's fine, thank God — bruises only. What I want is the landlord forced to fix the playground and the other hazards we've reported for a year; it's a habitability fight, not an injury case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She's fine — bruises. What I want is the landlord forced to fix the playground and the hazards we've reported for a year.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an injury claim exists or repairs/habitability is the goal
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: She fractured her arm and needed surgery — this is very much an injury claim against the complex.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She fractured her arm and needed surgery — it's an injury claim.
scenarios.vetting_note: Injury-severity/remedy fact: prevention demand vs compensation claim.

scenarios.opening_query: A city bus jolted so hard that I was thrown into the fare box and knocked out cold.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Tort Claims Act || General Litigation > Personal Injury
scenarios.hidden_fact: The jolt was a pickup truck running a red light into the bus — the private driver is the at-fault party, and his insurer has already contacted me.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: A pickup ran a red light into the bus — the private driver's at fault and his insurer already contacted me.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a private third party rather than the transit agency caused the crash
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No other vehicle — the driver was texting and slammed the brakes for a missed stop; transit's own incident report says so.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No other vehicle — their own report says the driver was texting and slammed the brakes.
scenarios.vetting_note: Reverse of the govt-defendant default: private tortfeasor removes the TCA overlay.
